title: Add depth sweep for logic-term nesting (unify, walk over nested compound terms)
goal: Filed by the P6 depth-coverage auditor (docs/DEPTH_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md). The logic-engine ops unify and walk recurse structurally over nested compound terms (a term containing terms containing logic vars, to arbitrary depth). This is genuine depth-dependent recursion, yet no P6 pillar sweeps it — a unification/occurs-check or walk-resolution bug that only bites at term-nesting depth N would be invisible. Add a depth d=1..N sweep that constructs nested compound terms with logic variables, runs unify + walk, and verifies the resolved substitution against a closed-form expectation at each depth (and that occurs-check / cyclic terms degrade cleanly, never silently). The other consciousness-engine ops (make-kb, factor graphs, workspaces, DNC/SDNC) are classified leaf (opaque handles) and are intentionally out of scope. On landing, assign a pillar to ESHKOL_UNIFY_OP and ESHKOL_WALK_OP in scripts/depth_coverage_registry.json.
acceptance: unify + walk over nested compound terms swept d=1..N, each depth verified against closed-form substitution; cyclic/occurs-check cases degrade cleanly
registry updated: unify + walk carry a pillar (not a gap_task)
